claim: Sequential agentic workflows follow concrete steps: Trigger → Retrieve → Agentic → Act/Notify → Learn; this structure enables building autonomous systems
actionable: Structure your agentic workflows as sequential steps; each step has clear inputs/outputs enabling modularity and debugging
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Living software: agentic workflows run unattended, detect changes in external data, and automatically update content—no manual intervention needed
actionable: Design workflows to run on timers; integrate change detection and automatic updates so your system evolves without manual prompting
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Change detection strategy: compare scraped current values against previously stored results using LLM or code; feed deltas to downstream systems
actionable: Implement change detection in your workflows: diff current state vs. previous, summarize changes, gate downstream actions on deltas
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Notification service (ntfy.sh) enables mobile alerts for workflow events; this observability is critical for debugging and understanding autonomous agent behavior
actionable: Add notifications to your agentic workflows; track when agents detect changes, what decisions they make, and what actions they take
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Prompt-driven filtering: instead of hardcoding keyword matching, pass context to LLM and let it filter based on learned feedback—more flexible and maintainable
actionable: Avoid hardcoded filtering logic; use prompts with context to enable data-driven filtering that improves over time
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Feedback loops: user thumbs-up/down on agent outputs refines subsequent runs; store feedback in config files that are re-injected into prompts
actionable: Capture user feedback on agent outputs; include feedback history in subsequent prompts so agents learn from user preferences
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Five tips for agentic workflows: 1) Be incremental, 2) Great logging/monitoring, 3) Use SOTA models first, 4) LLMs are functions, 5) Build for tomorrow
actionable: Start simple, add monitoring, use best models, iterate incrementally, and plan for expanding agent autonomy over time
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Future trend: as models improve, agents will write less code and delegate more work; conditional logic and loops will shift to agent responsibility
actionable: Design systems to gradually shift control logic from code to prompts; use agents for conditionals and loops where appropriate
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Python Schedule library enables simple, readable cron-like scheduling for triggering workflows; preferred over cron for code-based triggers
actionable: Use Python Schedule for workflow triggers; it's more readable than cron and integrates cleanly with Python code
source_title: USEFUL Agentic Workflow: AUTO-Updating Blog with Claude 3.5 Sonnet
